supply_chain.dependencies: Relies on external infrastructure, proprietary information technology and third party systems and services including cloud service providers for business operations
risks: Loss of or disruption in key vendor relationships, failure of vendors to protect customer/employee information, cybersecurity threats from vendors, reliance on third party service providers for critical operations
Cybersecurity threats, operational risks from third-party vendors, technology system failures

regulatory_risks: U.S. federal and state insurance regulations
International insurance regulations in operating jurisdictions
Environmental laws (U.S. federal, state, local and international)
AI regulations (EU AI Act, Colorado AI Act)
Banking regulations (FDIC, Office of the Comptroller of the Currency)
Tax law changes (U.S. federal, state, international, OECD base erosion and profit shifting)
Corporate alternative minimum tax (CAMT) under IRA 2022
International Association of Insurance Supervisors (IAIG) framework
International regulatory requirements in Chile, Mexico, Hong Kong, Brazil
Pillar Two global minimum tax (15% OECD rules)
Solvency regulations under Iowa Insurance Law
NAIC risk-based capital (RBC) requirements and reserve standards
Changes in insurance laws and regulations
Bermuda Monetary Authority (BMA) regulatory approval requirements
Iowa insurance holding company laws and dividend restrictions
ERISA and DOL regulations for employee benefit plans
Inflation Reduction Act of 2022 (corporate alternative minimum tax, excise tax on stock repurchases)
Privacy and data protection laws (EU GDPR, CCPA, CPRA, NY DFS Part 500, China Cybersecurity Law, China Personal Information Protection Law)

sanction_risks: Exchange controls or restrictions preventing fund transfers from foreign operations
Risk of discriminatory regulation, nationalization or expropriation of assets in international operations
Rising geopolitical tension and competing legal requirements between U.S. and other countries such as China
Political instability and social unrest in international markets
Judicial and regulatory reforms in Mexico creating uncertainty for investors regarding rule of law
Increased strategic competition between U.S. and China may negatively impact international businesses
Chilean government pension system reform proposals
